channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Effectiveness of an individualised swimming and education programme for chronic low back pain (EduSwim): a randomised controlled trial.
item.description: This RCT (n=?) compared an individualized swimming and education program (8 weeks via 4 telehealth PT sessions) with education alone for chronic low back pain, finding significant improvement in disability (RMDQ) at 8 weeks for the intervention group.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Virtual Reality-assisted Physiotherapeutic Training for Patients With Knee Osteoarthritis: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 5 RCTs found that virtual reality-assisted physiotherapy significantly improved pain (SMD = 0.40, 95% CI: 0.14-0.65, P = 0.002), physical function (SMD = 0.37, 95% CI: 0.05-0.70, P = 0.003), and balance (SMD = 0.44, 95% CI: 0.16-0.72, P = 0.002) in patients with knee osteoarthritis compared to traditional rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Association of combined physical and cognitive rehabilitation with post-stroke dementia: a U.S. multicenter retrospective cohort study.
item.description: A U.S. multicenter retrospective cohort study of 958 adults aged ≥55 years with stroke and MCI found that combined physical and cognitive rehabilitation significantly reduced the risk of developing post-stroke dementia compared with physical rehabilitation alone (HR = 0.70, 95% CI: 0.55-0.89).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Oxford visual perception screen detects visual perception difficulties in sub-acute adult stroke survivors: an early cross-sectional diagnostic accuracy study.
item.description: A prospective cross-sectional study in three UK stroke rehabilitation units found that the Oxford Visual Perception Screen (OxVPS) effectively detects visual perception difficulties in sub-acute adult stroke survivors, showing good diagnostic accuracy compared to the Rivermead Perceptual Assessment Battery (RPAB).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: Implementation of the Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) bundled payment model in the US led to a significant decrease in the proportion of hospitals delivering prehabilitation for lower extremity joint replacement, with a difference-in-differences analysis showing a clear negative effect.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Beyond "Biomedical-Plus": Reclaiming the biopsychosocial model in musculoskeletal physiotherapy.
item.description: This perspective piece argues that the biopsychosocial (BPS) model in musculoskeletal physiotherapy often defaults to a "biomedical-plus" approach, suggesting that deeper integration requires professional confidence, role clarity, and compassionate communication (the 3Cs).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effectiveness of Combined Biopsychosocial Interventions for Chronic Low Back Pain: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis evaluated combined biopsychosocial interventions for chronic low back pain, showing effectiveness in reducing pain and functional disability compared to usual care, waiting lists, or unimodal interventions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Comparative efficacy of different joint mobilization techniques in shoulder adhesive capsulitis: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis compared the clinical effects of different non-thrust joint mobilization approaches for shoulder adhesive capsulitis, analyzing randomized controlled trials published between 2000 and 2025.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The RESOLVE Trial: A Stepped-Wedge, Cluster-Randomized Clinical Trial Evaluating the Effects of Clinical Practice Guidelines and Psychologically Informed Physical Therapy on Low Back Pain Outcomes in Service Members and Veterans.
item.description: A stepped-wedge, cluster-randomized trial in military and veteran healthcare settings found that a combined clinical practice guideline (CPG) and psychologically informed physical therapy (PIPT) implementation strategy significantly reduced LBP-related disability, pain, and downstream healthcare utilization.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Measurement Characteristics and Clinical Utility of the 6-Minute Walk Test Among People with Stroke
item.description: This study aims to evaluate the measurement characteristics and clinical utility of the 6-Minute Walk Test (6MWT) among people with stroke.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: A systematic review of current evidence on clinical outcomes of lumbar roll and modified SIMS manipulation techniques in sacroiliac joint dysfunction.
item.description: This systematic review aims to evaluate the clinical efficacy of lumbar roll and modified SIMS manipulation techniques for sacroiliac joint dysfunction (SIJD), searching studies published between January 2021 and December 2025.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: In adult patients with brainstem strokes, does dysphagia rehabilitation improve health-related outcomes compared to usual care for inpatients? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis (12 studies, 443 participants) synthesized evidence on dysphagia rehabilitation for inpatients with brainstem stroke, categorizing interventions into traditional swallowing therapy or device inclusion, finding overall effectiveness.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Long-term benefits of ultrasound-guided percutaneous electrical nerve stimulation for chronic lateral elbow pain: a randomized controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized, double-blinded, sham-controlled trial with 49 participants found that ultrasound-guided percutaneous electrical nerve stimulation applied to the radial nerve, combined with a 4-week exercise program, provided mid- and long-term benefits for chronic lateral elbow pain.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: A Multimodal Assessment of Gastric Motility, reflux risk, and Clinical Efficacy: Intermittent Oro-Esophageal versus Nasogastric Tube Feeding for Post-Stroke Dysphagia in a Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A single-center RCT in China with 54 post-stroke dysphagia patients found that intermittent oro-esophageal tube feeding (IOE) for 14 days was superior to continuous nasogastric tube feeding (NG) in reducing reflux risk and supporting swallowing recovery.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effectiveness of different modes of digital health interventions for sarcopenia in older adults: a systematic review and network meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and network meta-analysis evaluated the effectiveness of different modes of digital health interventions for sarcopenia in older adults.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Outpatient rehabilitation can prevent suicide after acquired brain injury.
item.description: A registry study of 591 patients with acquired brain injury found that outpatient rehabilitation can prevent suicide, showing a higher frequency of suicide after stroke and traumatic brain injury than in the general population.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: [Correspondence] From targets to minutes: making movement advice actionable
item.description: This correspondence emphasizes shifting physical activity messaging from aspirational thresholds to achievable marginal gains, suggesting that small increases in activity among the least active yield the greatest population benefit, aligning with WHO guidance.
item.author: The Lancet

item.title: From mechanics to mobilization: a biomechanically-informed rehabilitation framework for cesarean-related scars
item.description: A systematic review proposed a biomechanically-informed rehabilitation framework for cesarean-related scars, integrating strategies for both abdominal wall and uterine scars, addressing a lack of standardization in current approaches.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Exploring equity in telehealth interventions in physiotherapy: A meta-epidemiological study.
item.description: A meta-epidemiological study assessed equity in physiotherapy telehealth interventions using the PROGRESS-Plus framework, including 94 telehealth equity trials, to understand factors influencing health outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Responsiveness of the Arabic version of the lower extremity functional scale in individuals with lower extremity musculoskeletal disorders.
item.description: This observational study evaluated the responsiveness of the Arabic version of the Lower Extremity Functional Scale (LEFS) in 74 individuals with lower extremity musculoskeletal disorders, finding its change scores correlated with other outcome measures.
item.author: PubMed
